item.title: Interplay of Kondo coupling and geometrical frustration in weakly coupled ruthenate pyrochlore ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{1.1}{\mathrm{Nd}}_{0.9}{\mathrm{Ru}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$
item.description:
Author(s): Sairam Ithineni, Soumyakanta Panda, Hanuma Kumar Dara, Ratiranjan Samal, Ajaya K. Nayak, Niharika Mohapatra, Diptikanta Swain, and D. Samal

The systems exhibiting coexistence of Kondo coupling and magnetic frustration offer a rich platform for exploring the interplay between these competing effects, which often give rise to exotic quantum phases. Rare-earth-doped metallic pyrochlores with noncoplanar spin configurations, pronounced Isin…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214457] Published Tue Dec 30, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sairam Ithineni, Soumyakanta Panda, Hanuma Kumar Dara, Ratiranjan Samal, Ajaya K. Nayak, Niharika Mohapatra, Diptikanta Swain, and D. Samal

The systems exhibiting coexistence of Kondo coupling and magnetic frustration offer a rich platform for exploring the interplay between these competing effects, which often give rise to exotic quantum phases. Rare-earth-doped metallic pyrochlores with noncoplanar spin configurations, pronounced Isin…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214457] Published Tue Dec 30, 2025
item.dc:title: Interplay of Kondo coupling and geometrical frustration in weakly coupled ruthenate pyrochlore ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{1.1}{\mathrm{Nd}}_{0.9}{\mathrm{Ru}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$
item.dc:creator: Sairam Ithineni, Soumyakanta Panda, Hanuma Kumar Dara, Ratiranjan Samal, Ajaya K. Nayak, Niharika Mohapatra, Diptikanta Swain, and D. Samal

item.title: Role of transition metal based kagome lattice in the physical properties of ${\mathrm{LaIr}}_{5}, {\mathrm{LaIr}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}, {\mathrm{LaIr}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ga}}_{2}, {\mathrm{LaRh}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$, and ${\mathrm{YRu}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$ superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): H. Y. Uzunok, S. Baǧcı, İ. Sümer, K. Başhan, and H. M. Tütüncü

Recent research on kagome compounds has pointed out their multiple applications in diverse fields. Here, we have reported the physical properties of hexagonal superconductors ${\mathrm{LaIr}}_{5}, {\mathrm{LaIr}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}, {\mathrm{LaIr}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ga}}_{2}, {\mathrm{LaRh}}_{3}{\mathrm{…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214520] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): H. Y. Uzunok, S. Baǧcı, İ. Sümer, K. Başhan, and H. M. Tütüncü

Recent research on kagome compounds has pointed out their multiple applications in diverse fields. Here, we have reported the physical properties of hexagonal superconductors LaIr5, LaIr3B2, LaIr3Ga2, LaRh3B2, and YRu3B2, in which transition metal atoms form a kagome lattice. In our ab initio calcul…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214520] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.dc:title: Role of transition metal based kagome lattice in the physical properties of ${\mathrm{LaIr}}_{5}, {\mathrm{LaIr}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}, {\mathrm{LaIr}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ga}}_{2}, {\mathrm{LaRh}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$, and ${\mathrm{YRu}}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$ superconductors
item.dc:creator: H. Y. Uzunok, S. Baǧcı, İ. Sümer, K. Başhan, and H. M. Tütüncü
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Noncollinear magnetic multipoles in collinear altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Luca Buiarelli, Rafael M. Fernandes, and Turan Birol

Altermagnets host an array of magnetic multipoles, which are often visualized and studied in reciprocal space. In real space, the relative phase of the multipoles of the spin density around atoms determines whether a system is an altermagnet or a conventional antiferromagnet. In this study, we appro…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224442] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Luca Buiarelli, Rafael M. Fernandes, and Turan Birol

Altermagnets host an array of magnetic multipoles, which are often visualized and studied in reciprocal space. In real space, the relative phase of the multipoles of the spin density around atoms determines whether a system is an altermagnet or a conventional antiferromagnet. In this study, we appro…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224442] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.dc:title: Noncollinear magnetic multipoles in collinear altermagnets
item.dc:creator: Luca Buiarelli, Rafael M. Fernandes, and Turan Birol
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Electronic structure and phonon properties in the kagome metals $R{T}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$ ($R=\mathrm{Y},\mathrm{Lu}$; $T=\mathrm{Co},\mathrm{Os}$)
item.description:
Author(s): Savita Chaudhary, Armando Consiglio, Jaskaran Singh, Domenico Di Sante, Ronny Thomale, and Yogesh Singh

The $R{T}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$ ($R=\mathrm{Y},\mathrm{Lu}, T=\mathrm{Co},\mathrm{Os}$) family hosts a perfect kagome lattice of $T$ atoms, offering an interesting platform to investigate the interplay of electronic structure, superconductivity, and lattice dynamics. Here, we compare two members of t…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235170] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Savita Chaudhary, Armando Consiglio, Jaskaran Singh, Domenico Di Sante, Ronny Thomale, and Yogesh Singh

The RT3B2 (R=Y,Lu, T=Co,Os) family hosts a perfect kagome lattice of T atoms, offering an interesting platform to investigate the interplay of electronic structure, superconductivity, and lattice dynamics. Here, we compare two members of this family, LuOs3B2 and YCo3B2, with similar crystallography …


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235170] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.dc:title: Electronic structure and phonon properties in the kagome metals $R{T}_{3}{\mathrm{B}}_{2}$ ($R=\mathrm{Y},\mathrm{Lu}$; $T=\mathrm{Co},\mathrm{Os}$)
item.dc:creator: Savita Chaudhary, Armando Consiglio, Jaskaran Singh, Domenico Di Sante, Ronny Thomale, and Yogesh Singh

item.title: Topological flat-band engineering in frustrated graphene antidot lattices
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaoyi Yuan, Jiawei Xia, and Shuze Zhu

Realizing materials with intrinsic flat bands holds significant potential for accessing correlated and topological phases. In this work, via large-scale tight-binding calculations, we demonstrate that pairing frustrated quantum antidots in a hexagonal lattice yields intrinsically tunable flat bands.…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235435] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaoyi Yuan, Jiawei Xia, and Shuze Zhu

Realizing materials with intrinsic flat bands holds significant potential for accessing correlated and topological phases. In this work, via large-scale tight-binding calculations, we demonstrate that pairing frustrated quantum antidots in a hexagonal lattice yields intrinsically tunable flat bands.…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235435] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.dc:title: Topological flat-band engineering in frustrated graphene antidot lattices
item.dc:creator: Xiaoyi Yuan, Jiawei Xia, and Shuze Zhu

item.title: Spin stripes in the Hubbard model: A combined dynamical mean-field theory and Bethe-Salpeter analysis
item.description:
Author(s): Ruslan Mushkaev, Francesco Petocchi, Shintaro Hoshino, and Philipp Werner

The Hubbard model is known to accommodate various electronic orders, including stripes, which are important for understanding the physics of cuprates. We study spin-stripe order in the square lattice Hubbard model as a function of doping and temperature, by solving the Bethe-Salpeter equation with t…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245165] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ruslan Mushkaev, Francesco Petocchi, Shintaro Hoshino, and Philipp Werner

The Hubbard model is known to accommodate various electronic orders, including stripes, which are important for understanding the physics of cuprates. We study spin-stripe order in the square lattice Hubbard model as a function of doping and temperature, by solving the Bethe-Salpeter equation with t…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245165] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.dc:title: Spin stripes in the Hubbard model: A combined dynamical mean-field theory and Bethe-Salpeter analysis
item.dc:creator: Ruslan Mushkaev, Francesco Petocchi, Shintaro Hoshino, and Philipp Werner

item.title: Noncentrosymmetric Mott ground state in the breathing kagome lattice ${\mathrm{Nb}}_{3}{\mathrm{Cl}}_{8}$
item.description:
Author(s): Keyi Li, Xin Han, Hao Li, Helin Mei, Mingshu Tan, Bingru Lu, Xiang Li, Wenbo Sang, Xinbo Wang, Anmin Zhang, Jianting Ji, Youguo Shi, Feng Jin, and Qingming Zhang

The breathing Kagome material ${\mathrm{Nb}}_{3}{\mathrm{Cl}}_{8}$ has recently been identified as a single-band Mott insulator and theoretically predicted to host coexisting ferroelectricity, magnetism, and ferrovalley—yet experimental verification of these predictions remains lacking. Here, throug…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214116] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Keyi Li, Xin Han, Hao Li, Helin Mei, Mingshu Tan, Bingru Lu, Xiang Li, Wenbo Sang, Xinbo Wang, Anmin Zhang, Jianting Ji, Youguo Shi, Feng Jin, and Qingming Zhang

The breathing Kagome material Nb3Cl8 has recently been identified as a single-band Mott insulator and theoretically predicted to host coexisting ferroelectricity, magnetism, and ferrovalley—yet experimental verification of these predictions remains lacking. Here, through polarization- and temperatur…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 214116] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.dc:title: Noncentrosymmetric Mott ground state in the breathing kagome lattice ${\mathrm{Nb}}_{3}{\mathrm{Cl}}_{8}$
item.dc:creator: Keyi Li, Xin Han, Hao Li, Helin Mei, Mingshu Tan, Bingru Lu, Xiang Li, Wenbo Sang, Xinbo Wang, Anmin Zhang, Jianting Ji, Youguo Shi, Feng Jin, and Qingming Zhang

item.title: Kitaev interaction and possible spin liquid state in ${\mathrm{CoI}}_{2}$ and ${\mathrm{Co}}_{2/3}{\mathrm{Mg}}_{1/3}{\mathrm{I}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yaozhenghang Ma, Ke Yang, Yuxuan Zhou, and Hua Wu

Kitaev materials are of great interest due to their potential in realizing quantum spin liquid (QSL) states and applications in topological quantum computing. In the pursuit of realizing Kitaev QSL, a Mott insulator with strong bond-dependent frustration and weak geometric frustration is highly desi…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, L220412] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yaozhenghang Ma, Ke Yang, Yuxuan Zhou, and Hua Wu

Kitaev materials are of great interest due to their potential in realizing quantum spin liquid (QSL) states and applications in topological quantum computing. In the pursuit of realizing Kitaev QSL, a Mott insulator with strong bond-dependent frustration and weak geometric frustration is highly desi…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, L220412] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.dc:title: Kitaev interaction and possible spin liquid state in ${\mathrm{CoI}}_{2}$ and ${\mathrm{Co}}_{2/3}{\mathrm{Mg}}_{1/3}{\mathrm{I}}_{2}$

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Physical properties and first-principles calculations of an altermagnet candidate ${\mathrm{Cs}}_{1−δ}{\mathrm{V}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{2}\mathrm{O}$
item.description:
Author(s): Chang-Chao Liu, Jing Li, Ji-Yong Liu, Jia-Yi Lu, Hua-Xun Li, Yi Liu, and Guang-Han Cao

We report the crystal growth, structure, physical properties, and first-principles calculations of a vanadium-based oxytelluride ${\mathrm{Cs}}_{1−δ}{\mathrm{V}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{2}\mathrm{O}$. The material possesses two-dimensional ${\mathrm{V}}_{2}\mathrm{O}$ square nets sandwiched by tellurium l…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224439] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chang-Chao Liu, Jing Li, Ji-Yong Liu, Jia-Yi Lu, Hua-Xun Li, Yi Liu, and Guang-Han Cao

We report the crystal growth, structure, physical properties, and first-principles calculations of a vanadium-based oxytelluride Cs1−δV2Te2O. The material possesses two-dimensional V2O square nets sandwiched by tellurium layers, with local crystallographic symmetry satisfying the spin symmetry for a…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 224439] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.dc:title: Physical properties and first-principles calculations of an altermagnet candidate ${\mathrm{Cs}}_{1−δ}{\mathrm{V}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{2}\mathrm{O}$
item.dc:creator: Chang-Chao Liu, Jing Li, Ji-Yong Liu, Jia-Yi Lu, Hua-Xun Li, Yi Liu, and Guang-Han Cao

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: High Chern number quantum anomalous Hall states in Haldane-graphene multilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Yuejiu Zhao, Long Zhang, and Fu-Chun Zhang

We consider a rhombohedral-stacked $N$-layer graphene coupled to a monolayer of Haldane model. We show that high-order Dirac points in multilayer graphene can be gapped out by the topological proximity effect of the Haldane model layer, leading to total Chern number $|C|=N+1$ quantum anomalous Hall …


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235166] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuejiu Zhao, Long Zhang, and Fu-Chun Zhang

We consider a rhombohedral-stacked N-layer graphene coupled to a monolayer of Haldane model. We show that high-order Dirac points in multilayer graphene can be gapped out by the topological proximity effect of the Haldane model layer, leading to total Chern number |C|=N+1 quantum anomalous Hall stat…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235166] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.dc:title: High Chern number quantum anomalous Hall states in Haldane-graphene multilayers
item.dc:creator: Yuejiu Zhao, Long Zhang, and Fu-Chun Zhang

item.title: Fractal electronic transport in graphene Cantor structures by means of power spectrum analysis
item.description:
Author(s): R. Rodríguez-González, R. D. Valdez-Cepeda, and I. Rodríguez-Vargas

Graphene fractal structures are ideal systems to analyze the impact of self-similar geometries on physical properties. Here, we study the electronic transport in graphene Cantor structures. A transmission scheme for the electronic transport is used. In particular, the zero-temperature linear-regime …


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235431] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): R. Rodríguez-González, R. D. Valdez-Cepeda, and I. Rodríguez-Vargas

Graphene fractal structures are ideal systems to analyze the impact of self-similar geometries on physical properties. Here, we study the electronic transport in graphene Cantor structures. A transmission scheme for the electronic transport is used. In particular, the zero-temperature linear-regime …


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235431] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.dc:title: Fractal electronic transport in graphene Cantor structures by means of power spectrum analysis
item.dc:creator: R. Rodríguez-González, R. D. Valdez-Cepeda, and I. Rodríguez-Vargas

item.title: Superconductor-insulator transition in a two-orbital attractive Hubbard model with Hund's exchange
item.description:
Author(s): Laura Torchia and Massimo Capone

We study a two-orbital attractive Hubbard model with a repulsive Hund's exchange coupling $J$ as an idealized model for a two-band superconductor. This framework is motivated by systems where strong isotropic electron-phonon coupling drives the on-site Hubbard repulsion $U$ to negative while leaving…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235161] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Laura Torchia and Massimo Capone

We study a two-orbital attractive Hubbard model with a repulsive Hund's exchange coupling J as an idealized model for a two-band superconductor. This framework is motivated by systems where strong isotropic electron-phonon coupling drives the on-site Hubbard repulsion U to negative while leaving the…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235161] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.dc:title: Superconductor-insulator transition in a two-orbital attractive Hubbard model with Hund's exchange
item.dc:creator: Laura Torchia and Massimo Capone
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Intrinsic nonlinear valley Nernst effect
item.description:
Author(s): Xue-Jin Zhang, Jin Cao, Lulu Xiong, Hui Wang, Shen Lai, Cong Xiao, and Shengyuan A. Yang

Nonlinear thermoelectric responses provide a novel probe of valley physics. Here, the authors develop a theory for the intrinsic nonlinear valley Nernst effect, identify its quantum geometric origin in Berry connection polarizability dipole, demonstrate a generalized Mott relation to nonlinear Hall effect, and predict scaling law for nonlocal thermoelectric transport. These advances lay a foundational framework for nonlinear valley caloritronics.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235430] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xue-Jin Zhang, Jin Cao, Lulu Xiong, Hui Wang, Shen Lai, Cong Xiao, and Shengyuan A. Yang

Nonlinear thermoelectric responses provide a novel probe of valley physics. Here, the authors develop a theory for the intrinsic nonlinear valley Nernst effect, identify its quantum geometric origin in Berry connection polarizability dipole, demonstrate a generalized Mott relation to nonlinear Hall effect, and predict scaling law for nonlocal thermoelectric transport. These advances lay a foundational framework for nonlinear valley caloritronics.


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235430] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.dc:title: Intrinsic nonlinear valley Nernst effect
item.dc:creator: Xue-Jin Zhang, Jin Cao, Lulu Xiong, Hui Wang, Shen Lai, Cong Xiao, and Shengyuan A. Yang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Mesoscale variations of the chemical and electronic landscape on the surface of the Weyl semimetal ${\mathrm{Co}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sn}}_{2}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}$ visualized by ARPES and XPS
item.description:
Author(s): Sudheer Anand Sreedhar, Matthew Staab, Mingkun Chen, Robert Prater, Zihao Shen, Giuseppina Conti, Ittai Sidilkover, Zhenghong Wu, Eli Rotenberg, Aaron Bostwick, Chris Jozwiak, Hadas Soifer, Slavomir Nemsak, Sergey Y. Savrasov, Vsevolod Ivanov, Valentin Taufour, and Inna M. Vishik

The multiple crystalline terminations in magnetic Weyl semimetal ${\mathrm{Co}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sn}}_{2}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}$ display distinct topological and trivial surface states, which have successfully been distinguished experimentally. However, a model of pure terminations is known to be inadequate be…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245154] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sudheer Anand Sreedhar, Matthew Staab, Mingkun Chen, Robert Prater, Zihao Shen, Giuseppina Conti, Ittai Sidilkover, Zhenghong Wu, Eli Rotenberg, Aaron Bostwick, Chris Jozwiak, Hadas Soifer, Slavomir Nemsak, Sergey Y. Savrasov, Vsevolod Ivanov, Valentin Taufour, and Inna M. Vishik

The multiple crystalline terminations in magnetic Weyl semimetal Co3Sn2S2 display distinct topological and trivial surface states, which have successfully been distinguished experimentally. However, a model of pure terminations is known to be inadequate because these surfaces exhibit a high degree o…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 245154] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.dc:title: Mesoscale variations of the chemical and electronic landscape on the surface of the Weyl semimetal ${\mathrm{Co}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sn}}_{2}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}$ visualized by ARPES and XPS

item.title: Relevance of long-range screening in Mott transition examined via a hydrogen lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Zi-Jian Lang (郎子健), Sudeshna Sen, Pak Ki Henry Tsang, Kristjan Haule, Vladimir Dobrosavljević, and Wei Ku (顧威)

The Mott transition, a metal-insulator transition due to strong electronic interaction, is observed in many materials without an accompanying change of system symmetry. An important open question in Mott’s proposal is the role of long-range screening, whose drastic change across the quantum phase tr…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235158] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zi-Jian Lang (郎子健), Sudeshna Sen, Pak Ki Henry Tsang, Kristjan Haule, Vladimir Dobrosavljević, and Wei Ku (顧威)

The Mott transition, a metal-insulator transition due to strong electronic interaction, is observed in many materials without an accompanying change of system symmetry. An important open question in Mott’s proposal is the role of long-range screening, whose drastic change across the quantum phase tr…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235158] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.dc:title: Relevance of long-range screening in Mott transition examined via a hydrogen lattice
item.dc:creator: Zi-Jian Lang (郎子健), Sudeshna Sen, Pak Ki Henry Tsang, Kristjan Haule, Vladimir Dobrosavljević, and Wei Ku (顧威)

item.title: Self-consistent tight-binding calculations with extended Hubbard interactions in rhombohedral multilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Dongkyu Lee, Wooil Yang, Young-Woo Son, and Jeil Jung

We study the mean-field broken symmetry phases of charge neutral multilayer rhombohedral graphene within tight-binding approximations including self-consistent extended Hubbard interactions. We used onsite and intersite Hubbard interactions obtained from a newly developed first-principles calculatio…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235160] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dongkyu Lee, Wooil Yang, Young-Woo Son, and Jeil Jung

We study the mean-field broken symmetry phases of charge neutral multilayer rhombohedral graphene within tight-binding approximations including self-consistent extended Hubbard interactions. We used onsite and intersite Hubbard interactions obtained from a newly developed first-principles calculatio…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235160] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.dc:title: Self-consistent tight-binding calculations with extended Hubbard interactions in rhombohedral multilayer graphene
item.dc:creator: Dongkyu Lee, Wooil Yang, Young-Woo Son, and Jeil Jung

item.title: Exciton pairs coupled via long-living phonons and their superfluorescent markers
item.description:
Author(s): Vladimir Al. Osipov and Boris Fainberg

A system of several Wannier-Mott excitons interacting with phonons in a bulk material is considered. We show that strong exciton-phonon coupling causes the formation of a coherent two-exciton state—the exciton pair. Unlike the biexcitons, where the photons play the role of force carrier, the exciton…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235206] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Vladimir Al. Osipov and Boris Fainberg

A system of several Wannier-Mott excitons interacting with phonons in a bulk material is considered. We show that strong exciton-phonon coupling causes the formation of a coherent two-exciton state—the exciton pair. Unlike the biexcitons, where the photons play the role of force carrier, the exciton…


[Phys. Rev. B 112, 235206] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.dc:title: Exciton pairs coupled via long-living phonons and their superfluorescent markers
item.dc:creator: Vladimir Al. Osipov and Boris Fainberg
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Semiconductors I: bulk
